headline: National Security Council Act grants PM unchecked emergency powers
context: The National Security Council Act 2016 allows the Prime Minister to declare security areas without parliamentary approval, raising constitutional concerns.

cards.text: NSC Act 2016 lets PM declare security zones without parliamentary approval
cards.sub: Security forces gain arrest and seizure powers bypassing normal legal process

cards.h: NSC Act Section 18 authorises arrest without warrant in declared security areas
cards.s: Act bypasses Article 150 emergency provisions that require Agong's proclamation
cards.d: NSC Act Section 18 authorises arrest without warrant in declared security areas Act bypasses Article 150 emergency provisions that require Agong's proclamation

cards.h: Bar Council described NSC Act as creating a 'mini emergency' without checks
cards.s: Fundamental liberties suspended in security areas without judicial oversight
cards.d: Bar Council described NSC Act as creating a 'mini emergency' without checks Fundamental liberties suspended in security areas without judicial oversight

cards.h: Act used 4 times since 2016 affecting communities in Sabah and Johor
cards.s: Residents in declared areas reported restricted movement and property searches
cards.d: Act used 4 times since 2016 affecting communities in Sabah and Johor Residents in declared areas reported restricted movement and property searches

cards.text: Emergency powers without emergency declaration is constitutional circumvention
Like giving the school principal emergency powers that never expire. The emergency is whatever the principal says it is.
cards.sub: Emergency-equivalent powers without emergency declaration remove the constitutional checks that emergencies normally trigger

cards.text: Opposition filed constitutional challenge against NSC Act in 2023, still pending
